Chapter 4.2: The Outside

Go back to the Map

This is the Outside - or better yet, the rest of the world and firstly, the people of Marley. It was deemed completely destroyed by the presence of Titans and no one inside the Walls knew anything of. Far bigger the picture becomes when one comes to discover everything you've known is a lie and what awaits our protagonist is far more complex and painful than just Titans outside the walls. But is this really the full picture? Explore the map to find some details.

Location Details: Paradis Island

Isolated from the rest of the world by a vast ocean, this massive island serves as the final, fortified refuge for the Subjects of Ymir. Encircled by three colossal, concentric walls, its inhabitants live in ignorance of the world beyond, believing humanity outside has perished while a dark, hidden truth slumbers within the walls themselves.

Learn more about Paradis Island.

Enter the Walls again

Item Details: The "Flying Boat", the Seaplane

A revolutionary marvel of engineering crafted by the Azumabito family tethered to a desperate gamble. This aquatic aircraft represents a fragile alliance's last hope to soar in the sky and reach the architect of the world's end after the start of the Rumbling>.
Its design heavily draws from early 20th-century aviation, mirroring the bulky but functional aesthetics of vintage real-world seaplanes engineered for perilous oceanic crossings.

Learn more about the Flying Boat.

Location Details: The world map, the Planisphere

A chilling revelation painted on parchment, shattering a century of isolated ignorance. This expansive map unveils a world far larger and more advanced than the islanders ever conceived—a sprawling civilization unified only by its shared hatred for those living within the walls.
A closer inspection reveals a striking, deliberate design choice: the geopolitical boundaries and landmasses are a direct, upside-down, and horizontally flipped mirror of our own Earth's geography. The island's geography and its position in relation to the Marleyan mainland strongly mirror an inverted map of real-world Madagascar and the African coast. This deliberate geographic parallel grounds the isolated, otherworldly setting in a tangible reality.

Learn more about the Outside World and Marley.

Location Details: Liberio, Marleyan city.

Liberio is a Marleyan city located on the northeastern part of the mainland that contains an internment zone, a designated area of land for Eldians on the mainland to live in.

Learn more about Liberio.

We are inside the main city of Marley's influence strolling peacefully around its streets. What would we see? Colors, specifically of three kinds: white/gray, yeallow and red. Differentiating their statuses, people of Eldia are obliged to wear these armbadns at any given moment when outside. Even if the outside for them was most often still within the confinement of the internment zones.

When looking at Eldian's recognition armbands it's hard not to be reminded of Jewish identifying armbands, that Jewish people were forced to wear during World War 2. The differences in the design are not many as well and we are brought to wonder and reflect more and more on how much of this narrative is cutting through paper and/or screens to our own reality.

This is another crucial item that holds surprise and betrayal. In the confusion of being thrown inside another world and among other people but for a few familiar faces, we are wondering where our main character has disappeared until, in the middle of holding our breaths and after various uncatched hints, we are greeted with a more rugged and older version of Eren, enchanting the child-soldier Falco with words of sweetness and compassion-inducing. At last, he convinces him to send this letter to his "far beloved ones" which will turn the wheel of fate for Marley.

Many letters were sent from soldiers from the front, most of them to loved ones for a cherished notice of being alive or missing the other person. Surely, some letters from the front must have been less neutral, but this one starts with a sweet "Dearest parents".

Location Details: Fort Slava, military fortress under the Mid-East Allied Forces.

Fort Slava is a fortress under the control of the Mid-East Allied Forces which acts as a strategic defense for the naval station. This is the first time we see a different "enemy" after we left the walls and our protagonists with a question and disenchantment of freedom.

Learn more about Fort Slava.

After a temporal jump in the plot, we're suddenly watching soldiers fight in a war that looks much more realistic to what we're used from documentation pictures of real-world wars. We can soon see though, that children act as soldiers and Eldians are forced to sacrifice themselves in the name of the Marleyan nation in this battle against the Mid-East to conquer Fort Slava.

Much of the dynamics, artillery and feeling we can see come up on screen from the scenes are reminiscing of World War 1 documentation photography. Where time and lives were disrupted inside tunnels dug deep in the ground and long waiting times were spent to understand the next move of the enemy.

As anticipated before, new kinds of figures and tools emerge here. A combo of these two categories is the child-soldier, particularly in this case we see Gabi, one of the eligible child-soldiers who are selected to compete and win for the chance to inherit one of the Nine Titans, swinging a cluster of hand grenades made by herself while she stages a diversion acting like an escaping child victim.

Hand grenades were a very common hand artillery for soldiers to carry around. This one for example was a habitual piece in the military kit of german soldiers of Third Reich. This specific kind was often used as a "bunch" kept together by metal wires or ropes.

Another first we have here, we are witnessing new kinds of technologies and weaponized transportation methods. We see the kind of evolution the Outside world has followed and airships are one of the best exmples. Soon after, Eldians soldiers will be scariiced and turned into Titans to destroy the fortress next to other special Titans.

This is a very close example of airship from real life,.
